reasoning: The Lean signature captures the informal statement exactly: `card G = 351` matches "group of order 351"; the existential `∃ (p : ℕ) (P : Sylow p G), p.Prime ∧ (p ∣ card G) ∧ P.Normal` matches "has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order." The Mathlib `Sylow p G` structure (in `GroupTheory/Sylow.lean`) extends `Subgroup G`, so `P.Normal` correctly refers to the underlying subgroup being normal. The explicit conjunction of `p.Prime` and `p ∣ card G` is exactly the "prime p dividing its order" restriction from the informal text, and including `p ∣ card G` is mathematically faithful (it prevents the vacuous case where p is a prime not dividing |G|, where any Sylow p-subgroup is trivial/normal). Quantifier structure and types all match.
